violations.text: The RIDOH Certified Food Safety Manager (CFSM) Licenses associated with the facility was observed to be expired as of January 2025. Application for CFSM printed and supplied to the PIC at the time of inspection and to be submitted as soon as possible. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

Raw animal foods (hamburger beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the prep unit refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage and store3d in a manner to prevent cross-contamination. Beef was adjusted for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was less than 200 parts per million (ppm) (observed at 0 ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200-400 ppm. Per the PIC, all equipment to be sanitized in the dish machine. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. At the time of inspection, the container of chlorine sanitizing solution was observed empty. The bottle was changed and dishwasher was retested at the time of inspection at the proper range of 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The wall mounted chemical dispenser was observed not properly dispensing dish detergent or sanitizer chemical to create a proper sanitizing solution in the 3-bay sink. Call to be made for service and all equipment to be sanitized in the dish washer or the 3-bay sink using bleach solution until unit is serviced/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The floor in areas of the facility was observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Estblishment is to use the three compartment sink to wash, rinse, and sanitize until dish machine is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers in the walk-in cooler and freezer have an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024

The floors throughout the facility (primarily behind or under equipment) have an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the prep area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicers) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicers were moved to 3-bay sink to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2023

The presence of flying insects is not controlled as evidenced by accumulation of flying insects around the cloth/mop bucket area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2023
